title: Peppery Crisp Russian Melon Soup
description: A light and refreshing Russian-inspired soup combining sweet melon and crisp cucumber with a peppery kick, finished with dill and a hint of yogurt.
instructions: Heat a medium pot over medium heat and sauté the diced onion and minced garlic until translucent, about 2 minutes.
Add the diced cantaloupe and cucumber, cooking for an additional 3 minutes to let the flavors mingle.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, covering the pot for 5–7 minutes or until the melon softens slightly.
Season the soup with salt, freshly ground black pepper, cayenne pepper, and lemon juice, tasting as you go to balance sweetness and heat.
Remove the pot from the heat and allow the soup to cool for 5 minutes; this short rest helps retain the crispness of the melon and cucumber.
Stir in the Greek yogurt (or sour cream) until the soup is creamy and smooth; adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve the soup hot or slightly chilled, garnished with extra dill, chives, and a light dusting of black pepper.
Optionally, let the soup chill in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before serving to enhance the crisp texture.
